steps.title: Convert years to months for consistent units
steps.content:
The tree grows 69 cm every $m$ months. To find growth over $k$ years, we need to express both time periods in the same unit. Convert years to months:

$$k \text{ years} = k \times 12 \text{ months} = 12k \text{ months}$$

steps.title: Set up the rate and calculate total growth
steps.content:
The growth rate is $\frac{69}{m}$ cm per month. Over $12k$ months, the tree grows:

$$\text{Growth} = \frac{69 \text{ cm}}{m \text{ months}} \times 12k \text{ months} = \frac{69 \times 12k}{m} \text{ cm} = \frac{828k}{m} \text{ cm}$$

steps.title: Select the matching expression
steps.content: The expression $\frac{828k}{m}$ represents the total centimeters the tree grows every $k$ years. Looking at the answer choices, the one with the correct form (growth quantity divided by $m$ months, multiplied by $k$ years) is choice D — D is correct.
